expressions.english: Which day of the week shall we meet?

Which day of the week is it today?
What day is today?
What are you doing next Saturday?
expressions.notes:
다음 = next
주 = week
토요일 = Saturday
*월요일 = Monday, 화요일 = Tuesday, 수요일 = Wednesday, 목요일 = Thursday, 금요일 = Friday, 일요일 = Sunday
뭐 = what; something
뭐 is the contracted form of 무엇.

expressions.english: Can't we go some other day of the week?
expressions.notes:
다른 = other, another
요일날 is the same as 요일 and is often used in spoken language.
-(으)면 안 돼요? = Can't I...?

expressions.english: I really hate Mondays.
expressions.notes: 싫다 = to be not likable, to not like; to be unpleasant; to not enjoy, to hate

expressions.english: Which day of the week do you like most?
I am busy until next Tuesday.
I am usually free on Wednesdays.
expressions.notes:
주로 = usually
한가하다 = to be free (time-wise); to not have many things to do

expressions.english: Which day of the week is good to meet?
This year, my birthday is on a Sunday.
Do you know which day of the week Chuseok is on this year?
expressions.notes: 추석 is one of two major holidays in Korea. It is similar to Thanksgiving in the United States as it celebrates the yearly harvest.

expressions.english: I go to work at a different time every day of the week.

expressions.korean: 무슨 요일날 가서 무슨 요일날 돌아와 요?
expressions.english:
Which day of the week do you leave and which day do you come back?
Which day of the week are you going and which day are you coming back?
expressions.notes: 돌아오다 = to return, to come back

expressions.korean: 그 가게가 무슨 요일에 문 닫는지 알아 요?
expressions.english: Do you know which day of the week that store closes?
expressions.notes:
그 = the; that
가게 = store, shop
문 = door
닫다 = to close

expressions.korean: 혹시 지난 화요일에 대학로에 있지 않 았어요?
expressions.english: Were you, by any chance, in Daehangno last Tuesday?
expressions.notes:
혹시 = by any chance
지난 = last
대학로 = Daehangno
대학로 is an area in downtown Seoul that is famous for having many theaters.
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.korean: 저는 일요일에는 밖에 나가지 않고 잠 만 자요.
expressions.english: I don't go out on Sundays and I only sleep.

expressions.korean: 거기는 금요일 밤만 되면 사람이 너무 많아져요.
expressions.english: That place gets too crowded on Friday nights.

expressions.korean: 저는 이번 주에는 어떤 요일에 만나도 상관 없어요.
expressions.english: I don't mind what day of the week we meet this week.
expressions.notes:
이번 = this time
상관 없다 = to not matter

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 저랑 같은 요일, 같은 시간에 수업을 들어요.
expressions.english: He attends classes on the same day of the week at the same time that I do.
expressions.notes:
그 사람 = that person; he, she
같다 = to be the same
같은 is the adjective form of 같다.
수업을 듣다 = to attend a class, take a class

expressions.korean: 그 날은 제가 시간이 안 될 것 같은데, 다른 요일에 시간 돼는 때 있어요?
expressions.english: I don't think I will be able to make time that day, but is there any other day this week when you have time?
expressions.notes:
그 날 = that day, the day
시간이 되다 = to be able to make it; to have time
때 = time
